41-19.en: Supremely auspicious, without blame; peaceful and tranquil, free from peril.

58-25.transition: 兌 Duì (Joyous Lake) -> 無妄 Wú Wàng (Innocence)
58-25.zh: 結網得解，受福安坐，終无患禍。
58-25.en: The knotted net finds release; one receives blessing and sits at ease. In the end there is no trouble or calamity.

28-23.transition: 大過 Dà Guò (Great Exceeding) -> 剝 Bō (Splitting Apart)
28-23.zh: 廓落失業，跨禍度福，利無所得。
28-23.en: Vast and vacant, livelihood lost; straddling disaster, crossing toward fortune. Profit is nowhere to be found.

attempts.note: Literal English translation + style-as-suffix. No scene in the words.
attempts.prompt: Supremely auspicious, without blame; peaceful and tranquil, free from peril. Chinese ink painting style.

attempts.note: Auspicious tone -> calm shanshui. All 7 composition rules woven in.
attempts.prompt: Through a weathered stone gate, mossy steps descend like pale brushstrokes toward a still valley. In the midground a lone figure in a vermillion robe crosses a low bridge over calm water that mirrors the sky like spilled silver. Pine boughs frame the upper edge; terraced hills fade into amber dawn mist behind. Chinese ink painting.

attempts.note: Latches onto the stray word 'net' and inverts the meaning (verse is about release/ease).
attempts.prompt: The knotted net finds release; one receives blessing and sits at ease. In the end there is no trouble or calamity. Chinese ink painting style.

attempts.note: Blessing and ease -> a human figure at rest. The released net becomes a calm detail, not the subject.
attempts.prompt: Beneath a thatched eave framing the view, an old fisherman sits at ease on worn planks, a loosened net slack at his feet like unraveled ink threads. In the midground a calm inlet catches dawn light; a small boat drifts, its lantern a single amber spark. Reed banks and pale hills recede into mist beyond. Chinese ink painting.

attempts.note: Wanders into a pleasant prosperous village -> tone is exactly backwards (verse is loss and emptiness).
attempts.prompt: Vast and vacant, livelihood lost; straddling disaster, crossing toward fortune. Profit is nowhere to be found. Chinese ink painting style.

attempts.note: Loss and emptiness -> ominous tone -> atmospheric-night. The empty basket carries 'profit nowhere to be found'.
attempts.prompt: Inside a ruined gateway framing a vast empty courtyard, a single lantern gutters against the dark like a dying ember. Foreground: cracked flagstones and an overturned empty basket. Midground: a lone figure crosses a narrow plank between two pools of shadow. Far off, faint cold hills dissolve into black mist. One vermillion seal glows on a leaning post. Chinese ink painting.
